usage: write_sdf [-help] <file_name> [-abstracted_model] [-adjust_setuphold_for_zero_hold_slack] [-base_delay] [-celltiming {all none nochecks}] [-collapse_internal_pins] [-condelse] [-delimiter <char>] [-delta_delay] [-edges {edged library noedge check_edge}] [-exclude_cells <cell_list>] [-exclude_cells_keep_io_nets] [-exclude_cells_keep_top_level_nets] [-exclude_disabled_arcs] [-exclude_disabled_modes] [-exclude_whatif_arcs] [-filter] [-ideal_clock_network] [-include_variation] [-interconn {all none noport noinport nooutport}] [-map_file {file1 file2 ...}] [-max_view <viewName>] [-merge_arcs] [-min_period_edges {posedge negedge both none}] [-min_view <viewName>] [-no_condition] [-no_derate] [-no_escape] [-nonegchecks] [-precision <non_neg_integer>] [-process <string>] [-recompute_delay_calc] [-recompute_parallel_arcs] [-recrem {merge_always split merge_when_paired}] [-remashold] [-resort_values_min_to_max] [-scale <float>] [-setuphold {merge_always split merge_when_paired}] [-splitrecrem] [-splitsetuphold] [-target_application {sta verilog}] [-temperature <string>] [-timingcheck {order}] [-transform_out_to_out_arcs] [-typ_view <viewName>] [-version {2.1 3.0}] [-view <viewName>] [-voltage <string>]

options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: <file_name>
options.description: Specifies the name of the SDF output file.

options.name: -abstracted_model
options.description: Writes out a special SDF file for the Gate Level Simulation flow.

options.name: -adjust_setuphold_for_zero_hold_slack
options.description: Adjust hold timing check to meet non-negative slack.

options.name: -base_delay
options.description: Writes delay values that do not include the delta (SI cross-talk) delay component. By default, SDF delays include the base and delta components plus any applied derating.

options.name: -celltiming
options.description: Specifies which cell delays and timing checks to write out

options.name: -collapse_internal_pins
options.description: Collapses arcs across internal pins to a single primary input to primary output arc

options.name: -condelse
options.description: Writes CONDELSE constructs with the default value when a COND construct is written

options.name: -delimiter
options.description: Specifies the hierarchy divider character to use in the output file

options.name: -delta_delay
options.description: Writes out only delta delay (SI cross-talk) values to the SDF file.

options.name: -edges
options.description: {edged library noedge check_edge} Specifies the edges values

options.name: -exclude_cells
options.description: Specifies a list of leaf-cell or hierarchical instances whose timing data will be excluded from the the SDF file

options.name: -exclude_cells_keep_io_nets
options.description: By default, when the -exclude_cells option is used, any annotations within the excluded cell and any annotations to or from the excluded cell are filtered from the SDF. Using the -exclude_cells_keep_io_nets will restrict the filtering to just the annotations which are completely enclosed by the cell. Annotations to or from the cell will be preserved.

options.name: -exclude_cells_keep_top_level_nets
options.description: The -exclude_cells_keep_top_level_nets allows you to configure the handling of top-level interconnect annotations to an instance that has been excluded from the SDF via the -exclude _cells option. By default, no interconnect arcs to the instance are included. When -exclude_cells_keep_top_level_nets is specified, interconnect arcs which span to the ports of the instance will be included in the SDF. The delay annotated will be the worst-case delays of the top-level nets to/from the first level sink/source pins within the block that traverse a given input/output port of the instance respectively. See also the -exclude_cells_keep_io_nets option.

options.name: -exclude_disabled_arcs
options.description: Excludes disabled arcs in the SDF file

options.name: -exclude_disabled_modes
options.description: Excludes timing arcs that have been disabled due to an active library mode setting from appearing in the SDF file

options.name: -exclude_whatif_arcs
options.description: Excludes IO path arcs created by WhatIf commands

options.name: -filter
options.description: Converts all negative IOPATH delays to zero

options.name: -ideal_clock_network
options.description: Marks delays of delay arcs, that are on the ideal clock networks, as 0 in the SDF file

options.name: -include_variation
options.description: When the selected analysis type is SOCV, the -include_variation option can be used to export SDF delay and check values with the variation component. By default, SDF delays and check values will not have the mean + N*sigma value.

options.name: -interconn
options.description: {all none noport noinport nooutport} Specifies which interconnect delays to write

options.name: -map_file
options.description: Specifies a list of sdf map files

options.name: -max_view
options.description: Uses the late delay from the specified analysis view to populate the SDF max slot, when in multi-mode multi-corner analysis mode.

options.name: -merge_arcs
options.description: Merges two arcs that are related to the same internal pin and forms one timing arc

options.name: -min_period_edges
options.description: {posedge negedge both none} Specifies how to write out minimum PERIOD checks. Default: both.

options.name: -min_view
options.description: Uses the early delay from the specified analysis view to populate the SDF min slot, when in multi-mode multi-corner analysis mode.

options.name: -no_condition
options.description: Suppresses the COND statements in the SDF file

options.name: -no_derate
options.description: Writes non-derated delays to the Standard Delay Format (SDF) file. By default, the set_timing_derate command writes scaled delays to the SDF file.

options.name: -no_escape
options.description: Writes out object names without escaping hierarchical dividers

options.name: -nonegchecks
options.description: Converts all negative timing check values to 0.0

options.name: -precision
options.description: Controls the number of digits appearing after the decimal point in the output SDF file. The default precision is the value of the report_precision. The maximum precision that can be specified is 8.

options.name: -process
options.description: Specifies one or a combination of the following process names that will appear in the header of the generated SDF file

options.name: -recompute_delay_calc
options.description: Specifying the option -recompute_delay_calc instructs the software to recompute any necessary data required for a complete SDF file before exporting.

options.name: -recompute_parallel_arcs
options.description: Instructs the software to temporarily recompute delays with separate delay calculation for parallel arcs. This is recommended for SDF being used for simulation.

options.name: -recrem
options.description: {merge_always split merge_when_paired} Specifies whether Recovery and Removal constraints are merged into a unified RECREM construct - or kept as separate checks. Default: merge_always.

options.name: -remashold
options.description: Converts the SDF v2.1 unsupported REMOVAL to HOLD checks when used with the -version 2.1 option

options.name: -resort_values_min_to_max
options.description: Sorts values in the SDF delay triplets into ascending order

options.name: -scale
options.description: Specifies a multiplier to the delay values used in the SDF file

options.name: -setuphold
options.description: {merge_always split merge_when_paired} Determines how SETUP and HOLD timing checks are written

options.name: -splitrecrem
options.description: Splits the RECREM checks into a RECOVERY check and a REMOVAL check

options.name: -splitsetuphold
options.description: Splits the SETUPHOLD timing checks into separate SETUP and HOLD checks

options.name: -target_application
options.description: Allows customization of the SDF output for use with STA or Verilog applications

options.name: -temperature
options.description: Defines the temperature values that will appear in the header of the generated SDF file

options.name: -timingcheck
options.description: Sorts the check delays in OCV analysis

options.name: -transform_out_to_out_arcs
options.description: Writes out all output-to-output timing arcs as input-to-output arcs

options.name: -typ_view
options.description: Uses the late delay from the specified analysis view to populate the SDF typ slot

options.name: -version
options.description: Specifies whether to generate the SDF V2.1 or V3.0

options.name: -view
options.description: Uses the early and late delays for the specified analysis view to populate the min and max SDF triplet slots

options.name: -voltage
options.description: Defines the voltage values that will appear in the header of the generated SDF file
